description: AMM commands — create, deposit, withdraw, bid, vote, delete, clawback, and query Automated Market Maker pools on the XRP Ledger

userStories.title: amm create command
userStories.description: As a liquidity provider, I want to create a new AMM pool from the CLI so I can earn trading fees on my deposited assets.
acceptanceCriteria: Read AMMCreate docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/ammcreate.md before implementing
Create src/commands/amm.ts and register it in src/commands/index.ts and src/index.ts
xrpl amm create with: --asset <spec> (required), --asset2 <spec> (required), --amount <value> (required), --amount2 <value> (required), --trading-fee <n> (required, 0-1000)
Asset spec format: 'XRP' for XRP, 'CURRENCY/issuer' for IOU (e.g. USD/rIssuer). No MPT support.

--amount and --amount2 are plain numbers; asset type inferred from --asset/--asset2. XRP amounts in drops, IOU amounts in decimal units (e.g. --asset XRP --amount 1000000 = 1M drops; --asset USD/rIssuer --amount 10 = 10 USD)
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
After successful create, print AMM account address and LP token currency code
CLI exits with error if --trading-fee is outside 0-1000
CLI exits with error if both assets have the same spec
tests/e2e/amm/amm.validation.test.ts: invalid trading fee, same asset for both slots (no network)
tests/e2e/amm/amm.create.test.ts: create XRP/IOU pool; verify via amm info; --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: amm deposit command
userStories.description: As a liquidity provider, I want to deposit assets into an existing AMM pool so I can receive LP tokens representing my share.
acceptanceCriteria: Read AMMDeposit docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/ammdeposit.md before implementing

xrpl amm deposit with: --asset <spec> and --asset2 <spec> (required, identify AMM), --amount <value> (optional plain number, asset type inferred from --asset), --amount2 <value> (optional plain number, asset type inferred from --asset2), --lp-token-out <value> (optional plain number; CLI auto-fetches LP token currency/issuer via amm_info), --ePrice <value> (optional plain number), --for-empty (boolean flag for tfTwoAssetIfEmpty)

Mode inferred from flags: --amount+--amount2+--lp-token-out=tfLPToken; --amount+--amount2+--for-empty=tfTwoAssetIfEmpty; --amount+--amount2=tfTwoAsset; --amount+--lp-token-out=tfOneAssetLPToken; --amount+--ePrice=tfLimitLPToken; --amount only=tfSingleAsset
CLI exits with error if flag combination does not match any valid mode
Standard key material and output options
tests/e2e/amm/amm.deposit.test.ts: double-asset deposit; single-asset deposit; LP-token deposit; --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: amm withdraw command
userStories.description: As a liquidity provider, I want to withdraw assets from an AMM pool by returning LP tokens so I can recover my liquidity.
acceptanceCriteria: Read AMMWithdraw docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/ammwithdraw.md before implementing

xrpl amm withdraw with: --asset <spec> and --asset2 <spec> (required), --lp-token-in <value> (optional plain number; CLI auto-fetches LP token currency/issuer via amm_info), --amount <value> (optional plain number, asset type inferred from --asset), --amount2 <value> (optional plain number, asset type inferred from --asset2), --all (boolean flag for withdraw-all modes)

Mode inferred from flags: --lp-token-in only=tfLPToken; --all only=tfWithdrawAll; --all+--amount=tfOneAssetWithdrawAll; --amount only=tfSingleAsset; --amount+--amount2=tfTwoAsset; --amount+--lp-token-in=tfLimitLPToken
CLI exits with error if flag combination does not match any valid mode
Standard key material and output options
tests/e2e/amm/amm.withdraw.test.ts: LP-token withdraw (get both assets); single-asset withdraw; withdraw-all; --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: amm bid and amm vote commands
userStories.description: As an LP token holder, I want to bid on the AMM auction slot and vote on the trading fee so I can get discounted trades and influence pool economics.
acceptanceCriteria: Read AMMBid docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/ammbid.md before implementing
Read AMMVote docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/ammvote.md before implementing

xrpl amm bid with: --asset <spec> and --asset2 <spec> (required), --bid-min <value> (optional plain number; CLI auto-fetches LP token currency/issuer via amm_info), --bid-max <value> (optional plain number; same auto-fetch), --auth-account <address> (repeatable, max 4)
xrpl amm vote with: --asset <spec> and --asset2 <spec> (required), --trading-fee <n> (required, 0-1000)
CLI exits with error if --trading-fee outside 0-1000
CLI exits with error if more than 4 --auth-account values provided
Standard key material and output options on both
tests/e2e/amm/amm.bid.test.ts: bid on auction slot; --json, --dry-run
tests/e2e/amm/amm.vote.test.ts: vote on trading fee; verify fee updated via amm info; --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: amm delete, amm clawback, and amm info commands
userStories.description: As a developer or token issuer, I want to delete empty AMM pools, claw back tokens from pools, and query pool state so I can manage AMM lifecycle and verify pool data.
acceptanceCriteria: Read AMMDelete docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/ammdelete.md before implementing
Read AMMClawback docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/ammclawback.md before implementing
xrpl amm delete with: --asset <spec> and --asset2 <spec> (required); standard key material and output options

xrpl amm clawback with: --asset <spec> (required, token being clawed back; issuer must match signer), --asset2 <spec> (required), --holder <address> (required), --amount <value> (optional max clawback), --both-assets (boolean flag, sets tfClawTwoAssets)

xrpl amm info with: --asset <spec> and --asset2 <spec> (required); uses amm_info RPC; default output shows AMM account, pool balances, LP token supply, trading fee, auction slot; --json for raw output; --node supported
tests/e2e/amm/amm.delete.test.ts: create AMM, withdraw all, delete, verify amm info returns not-found; --json, --dry-run
tests/e2e/amm/amm.info.test.ts: create AMM, verify amm info shows correct balances and fee; --json
Typecheck passes
